MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Vacated

Motion To Stay Or In The Alternative To Consolidate And Stay
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen Mouritsen and Chad Pehrson, esq. (801-532-7840) of Parr Brown Gee & Loveless for Defendants Wakaya Perfection, LLC and Todd Smith via Court Call.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs TODD SMITH and WAKAYA PERFECTION, LLC's Motion To Stay Or In The Alternative To Consolidate And Stay.

Defendants Wakaya Perfection, LLC and Todd Smith's motion to consolidate this action with case No. CGC-17-557537 is granted. The cases are consolidated for all purposes with CGC-17-557537 as the lead case. The consolidated action is stayed pending the conclusion of the federal action between the parties in the Southern Disctrict of California, Case No. 3:16-CV-00704-L-JLB (S.D.Cal. filed March 23, 2016).

Currently there are three pending actions between the parties dealing in whole or in part with the issues of the presence of substances injurious to human health in defendant's products and defendant's compliance with California law as to the labelling of these products. Because the two state court actions pending before this court involve common questions of law and fact, they are consolidated for all purposes. Both actions concern defendant's alleged failure to comply with Cal. Health and Safety Code 25249.5 et seq. when marketing its products allegedly containing lead and arsenic (in the first action) and acrylamide or lead (in the second action) exceeding the statutory safe harbor. The actions should be litigated as one to avoid unnecessary inefficiencies. (See In re Marriage of Davis (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 294, 304 ["The law abhors multiplicity of actions, consuming the time of the court and entailing additional expense to the people."]; Simmons v. Superior Court (1950) 96 Cal.App.2d 119, 130 [" 'It is the policy of the law to reduce to the minimum the number of actions which may subsist between the same parties."].).

Although defendant's alternative motion to consolidate the actions was not filed in both actions as required by Rule 3.350(a) of the California Rules of Court, the irregularity is harmless and only as to form because all attorneys have been served and the motion includes the caption of both cases. Plaintiff has failed to show that failing to lift the stay imposed in the lead state court case would prejudice it with respect to its claims under this action because of undue delay. This action was filed in July 2018, while the federal action has already significantly advanced to the stage of fully briefed summary judgment motions after the completion of extensive discovery and there is a significant likelihood that the conclusion of the federal action would dispose of some of the claims in both state court actions. Accordingly, extending the previously ordered stay to both state court actions is appropriate. (See, e.g., Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 803-807 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in staying state court action in favor of federal action between substantially identical parties affecting the same subject matter as the state case where the federal case had been filed nearly nine months before the state court action].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Specially Appearing DEFENDANT TODD SMITH's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Provide Statutory Notice Or, Alternatively, To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Defendant Todd Smith's motion to dismiss for failure to provide statutory notice or, alternatively, to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is MOOT per the parties' joint motion to dismiss Todd Smith with prejudice filed with the court on January 16, 2019. The court will sign the proposed order for voluntary dismissal of Defendant Todd Smith with the change that he is dismissed with prejudice per the parties' stipulated motion.

Prevailing party to submit order.
